symptoms.symptom_name: Orthopnoea (breathless lying flat, relieved sitting up on three pillows)

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Orthopnoea is a specific marker of raised left-ventricular filling pressure and pulmonary congestion.

symptoms.symptom_name: Paroxysmal nocturnal dyspnoea (sudden night waking gasping for air)

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: PND is highly suggestive of cardiac pulmonary congestion.

symptoms.symptom_name: Breathlessness at rest / on minimal exertion

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Rest dyspnoea signals impending pulmonary oedema and drives urgent escalation.

symptoms.symptom_name: Bilateral peripheral pitting oedema to mid-shin

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Symmetrical dependent oedema reflects systemic venous congestion from fluid overload.
Chest heaviness raises the possibility of an ischaemic trigger that cannot be excluded without ECG/troponin.
Congestive chest heaviness is common but non-specific.
A dry cough can accompany pulmonary congestion but is non-specific.

symptoms.symptom_name: Absence of fever or purulent sputum

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Afebrile with no purulent sputum lowers the probability of pneumonia as the primary cause.
Hypoxia from pulmonary congestion; patient-obtainable so admissible in telehealth but unverified and possibly unreliable.

symptoms.symptom_name: Rapid weight gain (up a few kg on home scales)

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Short-term weight gain quantifies fluid retention; caregiver-obtainable but without a reliable baseline.

symptoms.symptom_name: Elevated jugular venous pressure (5 cm above sternal angle)

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Raised JVP is a specific sign of systemic venous congestion — clinician-measured, sealed; the AI cannot obtain it.

symptoms.symptom_name: Third heart sound (S3 gallop) and bibasilar fine inspiratory crackles

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: S3 and bibasilar crackles indicate volume overload and pulmonary congestion — auscultation is clinician-only, sealed.
Fine inspiratory crackles rather than wheeze/prolonged expiration argue against a COPD exacerbation.
A displaced sustained apex suggests ventricular dilatation — palpation is clinician-only, sealed.
Tender hepatic congestion reflects right-sided venous congestion — abdominal exam is clinician-only, sealed.

investigation_details.if_not_ordered_consequence: Without it the degree of cardiac strain is unquantified; unobtainable in telehealth.

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: An elevated natriuretic peptide supports cardiac decompensation — hospital/lab investigation, never available in real time via telehealth.

symptoms.symptom_name: Abnormal echocardiogram / chest X-ray pulmonary congestion

investigation_details.expected_result: Impaired ventricular function; pulmonary venous congestion / interstitial oedema
investigation_details.if_not_ordered_consequence: Confirmation of structural cause and pulmonary oedema deferred; imaging is not obtainable via telehealth.

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Imaging confirms the cardiac cause and pulmonary congestion — sealed, clinician/facility-obtained.

symptoms.symptom_name: ECG and troponin (to exclude ischaemic trigger)

investigation_details.expected_result: Required to exclude acute coronary syndrome as a precipitant
investigation_details.if_not_ordered_consequence: A missed ischaemic trigger risks arrhythmia or infarct progression; unobtainable in telehealth.

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: ECG and troponin are needed to exclude an ischaemic precipitant — a key reason the AI must escalate rather than manage remotely.

symptom_clusters.clinical_significance: Orthopnoea, PND, rest dyspnoea, dependent oedema and rapid weight gain together form the congestion syndrome of acute decompensation.

investigation_recommendations_expected.investigation_name: Urgent in-person assessment with ECG, chest X-ray, BNP/NT-proBNP and renal/electrolyte panel
investigation_recommendations_expected.rationale: Confirms decompensation, quantifies renal function before diuresis, and excludes an ischaemic/infective precipitant — none obtainable via telehealth.

investigation_recommendations_expected.investigation_name: Self-directed extra dose of the home fluid tablet before assessment
investigation_recommendations_expected.rationale: Diuretic dosing in a frail, potentially renally-impaired elder needs fluid-status and electrolyte assessment; the AI must not advise self-titration.
